name: Generating DEVS Code from DrawIO Diagrams
description: To generate DEVS Python code from a drawio diagram: (1) Parse the XML to identify modules (shape=module vertices), the coupled model container (shape=component), connections (edges with source/target), and labels (text vertices with message types in curly braces). (2) Map each module to an Atomic subclass. (3) Extract message types from edge labels (e.g., '{ AssignEmployee, HaltProduction }') and create @dataclass for each. (4) Create ports based on connections -- each edge label message type needs a port on both source (output) and target (input). (5) Wire connections in main.py using graph.connect(). (6) Required interface connectors (shape=requiredInterface) are external input ports; ellipse connectors are external output ports.

name: Factories-to-Company Translation Pattern
description: The factories example (CapitalProvider, Factory, ProductMarket) served as the reference implementation for the company example (Manufacturing, Administration, R&D). Key mappings: each drawio module becomes an Atomic subclass file, each message type in edge labels becomes a @dataclass in Messages.py, each edge becomes a graph.connect() call in main.py, external interface connectors become ports on atomic models without connections in standalone mode. The file structure mirrors factories: __init__.py, entity dataclasses (Employee.py, Product.py), Messages.py (all message types), one .py per atomic model, main.py for wiring.

content: When generating DEVS models from diagrams, if the same message type flows from different sources to the same target, create separate ports per source (e.g., REQUEST_EMPLOYEE_MFG_INPUT_PORT vs REQUEST_EMPLOYEE_RND_INPUT_PORT) to enable proper routing. Port indices must be unique within a direction (input or output) for each model.

Example DEVS models are placed under src/examples/<name>/ with this file structure: __init__.py (empty package marker), entity dataclasses (Employee.py, Product.py), Messages.py (all inter-model message @dataclasses grouped by category), one Python file per Atomic subclass named after the module, and main.py containing the simulation setup with AtomicGraph wiring and simulation loop.
